name: Custom Domains
description: BYO custom domains as a dregg-native cell: a domain binding is a factory-born sovereign cell whose committed slots {domain, site, owner, verification_state, challenge_nonce, verified_seq} carry the WriteOnce(domain/owner/challenge_nonce) + Monotonic(verification_state/verified_seq) invariants the executor re-enforces. A DomainCap-gated bind turn issues a DNS challenge; a verify turn flips verification_state once against an injected DnsResolver seam; site_for_host / is_verified are the verified reads a gateway consults. Register, bind, verify.

deos.mount: register_deos (src/lib.rs) — folds the composed DeosApp into the StarbridgeAppContext affordance registry; DeosApp::mount yields the axum router; DeosApp::publish_all mints the binding cell's dregg:// sturdyref
deos.publish: Signature (a gateway/resolver reacquires the binding cell across a federation membrane and reads the verified SITE the domain points at)

affordances.register: cap-only, None/root (the OWNER seals DOMAIN + OWNER — WriteOnce, no takeover)
affordances.bind: cap-only, None/root (the OWNER points at a site + seals the DNS challenge nonce — WriteOnce(CHALLENGE_NONCE); the fine bind-cap gate is the dregg-auth credential verify)
affordances.verify: GATED (cap∧state), None/root — not-yet-verified precondition (VERIFICATION_STATE==0); the fire flips VERIFICATION_STATE->1 and advances VERIFIED_SEQ off live state, the executor re-enforcing Monotonic on both
affordances.resolve: cap-only, Signature (a RESOLVER reads the verified SITE the domain points at)

deos.seam: CLOSED — the fire runs against the seeded domain cell carrying the binding program (WriteOnce(DOMAIN/OWNER/CHALLENGE_NONCE) + Monotonic(VERIFICATION_STATE/VERIFIED_SEQ)), so an UN-VERIFY (VERIFICATION_STATE 1->0), a rewound VERIFIED_SEQ, a re-issued challenge nonce, and an owner TAKEOVER all BITE in the deos fire path. The htmx tooth: after verify, the verify button goes DARK (VERIFICATION_STATE!=0 fails its precondition) — the domain flips to verified exactly once.
